title: RP_INITIAL – CPRIT Established - First Grants Awarded ($3B)
body: Voters approved Prop 15 authorizing $3B for cancer research. BCM and MD Anderson liver researchers were early recipients, launching Texas's HCC infrastructure.

title: El-Serag HB “Hepatocellular Carcinoma”
inst: Baylor College of Medicine / Houston VA
body: Landmark global HCC review documenting the rapid rise of HCV-related HCC as the fastest-growing cause of US cancer deaths.

title: Ramirez AG et al. “HCC Incidence & Risk Factors in Texas Latinos”
body: First study documenting South Texas Latinos have the nation's highest HCC rates (10.6/100K). Identified obesity and diabetes as key modifiable risk factors.

title: El-Serag HB “Epidemiology of Viral Hepatitis and HCC”
body: Quantified population-attributable fraction of HBV/HCV in HCC; foundational for Texas public health interventions.

title: Ramirez AG et al. “HCC in Texas Latinos 1995-2010: An Update”
body: Confirmed TX Latino male/female HCC rates 3.1x and 4.0x higher than non-Hispanic Whites.

title: RP150587 – Texas HCC Consortium (THCCC) — CPRIT Launch ($6.0M)
inst: BCM / VA / MD Anderson / UTSW / Parkland / UT San Antonio
body: CPRIT Multi-Investigator Award (RP150587) establishing the THCCC — now the largest ongoing prospective cohort of patients with cirrhosis in the nation, across 6 Texas centers.

title: CLAYTON_2016 – Clayton Foundation — HCC Biorepository & Treatment Research ($1.5M)
body: $300K/year for up to 5 years from the Clayton Foundation for Research. Develops an HCC biorepository and new treatment strategies; focused on the disproportionately high HCC burden in South Texas Hispanics.

title: Ramirez AG et al. “Lifestyle & Clinical Correlates of HCC - Aflatoxin Study”
body: First U.S. study linking aflatoxin exposure to HCC in a Latino-majority population. Cases had 6x higher odds of detectable aflatoxins in blood.

title: Tzartzeva K et al. “AFP + Ultrasound for HCC Detection: Meta-Analysis”
body: 32 studies: AFP + ultrasound improves HCC sensitivity to 63% vs 45% for ultrasound alone. Formed basis for updated AASLD surveillance guidelines.

title: THCCC Cohort - 2000+ Patients Enrolled
inst: BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio
body: Semi-annual ultrasound, AFP, labs, and biospecimens collected across 5 Texas sites. One of the largest prospective HCC biomarker repositories in the US.

title: Kulik L et al. “Epidemiology and Management of HCC”
body: Comprehensive review of HCC risk stratification, surveillance, staging, and treatment. Addressed NAFLD/NASH-HCC epidemic and rising rates in Hispanic populations.

title: TeCH_CAP_R1 – TeCH Center CAP Round 1 ($18.0M)
body: CPRIT Collaborative Action Program (CAP) award — up to $18M — establishing the Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH) as the coordinating hub for statewide HCC research.

title: RP190641 – HCC Biomarker Validation - El-Serag ($3.0M)
body: Biomarker validation grant for HCC early detection research within the THCCC cohort.

title: MDA_HCC_2019 – Patient-Centered Liver Cancer Prevention - Hwang ($2.5M)
body: Patient-centered liver cancer prevention initiative targeting at-risk populations at MD Anderson.

title: Feng Z et al. “Texas HCC Consortium (THCCC) Cohort Study Design”
inst: BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio
body: Describes the THCCC 5000-patient prospective cohort design and multi-center data collection protocol.

title: Wang CP et al. “Metformin Modifies HCC Disparity in Diabetic Men”
body: In 84433 men with T2D, metformin was associated with reduced HCC risk with stronger effects in Hispanic and African American men.

title: RP200537 – Cirrhosis Cohort Biomarker Surveillance - Singal ($2.5M)
body: 3000-patient cirrhosis cohort developing biomarker-based HCC surveillance algorithms.

title: RP200554 – Genetic Epidemiology of HCC in Hispanics - Thrift ($2.5M)
body: Genetic epidemiology of HCC in Hispanics; focused on PNPLA3 and TM6SF2 variants.

title: Singal AG et al. “Epidemiology & Surveillance for HCC: New Trends”
body: Documents shift from viral hepatitis to NAFLD/NASH as dominant HCC etiology; projects 122% increase in NAFLD-HCC by 2030.

title: Loomba R et al. “AGA Clinical Practice Update: HCC Screening & Surveillance in NAFLD”
inst: UCSD / Yale / Univ of Arizona / BCM
body: Joint AGA expert review on HCC screening in the rapidly growing NAFLD population. Addresses surveillance intervals and novel biomarkers.

title: PP220022 – Community HCC Prevention at Safety-Net Clinics ($2.0M)
body: Community-based HCC prevention initiative at safety-net clinics targeting underserved Texas populations.

title: RP220119 – GALAD + AFP-L3 Biomarker Validation - El-Serag ($2.0M)
body: Validation of GALAD score and AFP-L3 as early detection biomarkers within the THCCC cohort.

title: TeCH_CAP_R2 – TeCH Center CAP Round 2 ($18.0M)
body: TeCH Center renewal expanding NASH-HCC research and health equity initiatives across Texas institutions.

title: P01CA263025 – NCI P01 — THCCC Five-Year Research Program ($5.5M)
inst: BCM / VA / MD Anderson / UTSW / Parkland / UT San Antonio
body: NCI P01 CA263025 ($5.5M+, 5 years) funding five THCCC research projects: NAFLD risk factors, metabolic syndrome, circadian disruption, biomarker development, and randomized prevention trials.

title: Singal AG et al. “HCC Surveillance Improves Outcomes: Meta-Analysis”
body: 59 studies / 145396 patients: Surveillance yields 1.86x early-stage detection and 33% mortality reduction (HR 0.67).

title: Harrison AC et al. “The Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH)”
body: Describes TeCH's multi-institutional research, education, and advocacy framework targeting reduction of Texas liver cancer mortality by 2030.

title: TCH_HCC_2023 – Predictive Biomarkers for Pediatric HCC - Lopez-Terrada ($6.0M)
body: Predictive biomarkers for pediatric HCC and hepatoblastoma at Texas Children's Hospital.

title: UTSW_HCC_2023 – Molecular Mechanisms of HCC Progression - Zhong ($1.1M)
body: Investigation of molecular mechanisms driving HCC progression and therapeutic targets.

title: El-Serag HB et al. “Prevention of HCC - White Paper (TeCH Consortium)”
body: Consensus recommendations covering HBV vaccination, HCV cure, metabolic intervention, and health equity strategies for Hispanic Texas communities.

title: El-Serag HB et al. “HES v2.0 Outperforms GALAD for HCC Detection”
inst: Baylor College of Medicine / THCCC Network
body: Phase 3 biomarker evaluation: HES v2.0 (AFP + AFP-L3 + DCP + clinical variables) demonstrated superior sensitivity and specificity over GALAD for early HCC detection.

title: CPRIT Surpasses $4B + NCI SPORE Designation
body: CPRIT surpasses $4B total (~$59-77M to liver cancer). UT Southwestern receives the first Texas NCI SPORE for Liver Cancer with Singal, Yopp et al.

title: CPRIT Discloses $46M+ Cumulative HCC Investment
body: CPRIT disclosed more than $46M invested in HCC academic research and prevention since 2010 — reflecting the policy impact of Texas researchers documenting the state's outsized liver cancer burden. Announced alongside the Liver Cancer Collaborative Action Program (CAP) launching the TeCH Center.

title: San Antonio Liver Cancer Symposium — Inaugural Virtual Edition
inst: Mays Cancer Center, UT Health San Antonio
body: First SALCS, held virtually October 1–2, 2021. Modeled after the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium. 50+ speakers from MD Anderson, Mayo Clinic, NCI, and Northwestern; covered HCC epidemiology, pre-clinical, translational, survivorship, and pediatric HCC.

title: San Antonio Liver Cancer Symposium 2022
inst: Mays Cancer Center, UT Health San Antonio / Downtown San Antonio
body: Second SALCS, October 15–16, 2022, downtown San Antonio. Sessions: community liver cancer programs, personalized approaches for viral/non-viral HCC, current therapeutic landscape, and locoregional treatments (surgery, IR, transplant). Led to a special series in Annals of Palliative Medicine.

title: San Antonio Liver Cancer Symposium 2023
inst: Mays Cancer Center, UT Health San Antonio / Hilton San Antonio Hill Country
body: Third SALCS, October 20–21, 2023, Hilton San Antonio Hill Country. Focus on HCC disparities, NAFLD-HCC, and community programs for at-risk Hispanic populations in South Texas.

title: San Antonio Liver Cancer Symposium 2024
inst: Mays Cancer Center, UT Health San Antonio / Hilton Palacio del Rio
body: Fourth SALCS, October 11–12, 2024, Hilton Palacio del Rio, San Antonio. Sessions: pediatric liver cancer, future of HCC screening and surveillance, advances in surgery, transplantation, and interventional radiology, and caregiver support. UTHSA presentation: Amelie G. Ramirez (IHPR, UT Health San Antonio): "Hepatocellular Carcinoma in South Texas: Epidemiology and Risk Factors".

title: San Antonio Liver Cancer Symposium 2025
inst: Mays Cancer Center, UT Health San Antonio / Westin San Antonio North
body: Fifth SALCS, October 9–11, 2025, Westin San Antonio North. Annual gathering of doctors, researchers, medical students, and patient advocates advancing liver cancer care in Texas. UTHSA presentation: Edgar Muñoz (UT Health San Antonio): "Texas at the Epicenter: Updated Epidemiology of Liver Cancer's Multi-Factor Dynamics".
